content.full_text:
CITY CLERK

RECD

MAR 2 9 09 AM '90

VIA TELECOPY AND
U.S. MAIL

CITY OF CARSON

February 27, 1990

Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This is to formally request confirmation of telephone advice provided by you on
Tuesday, February 27, 1990, regarding Section 18901, Mass Mailings Sent at
Public Expense, given as follows in reference to the new interpretation that the
Sample Ballot is a form of the agency:

1. That it would not be allowable for the name of the city clerk to be
printed on the cover sheet page of the Sample Ballot.

2. That it would be allowable for the name of the city clerk to be
printed as the return addressee of the Sample Ballot.

3. That it would be allowable for the name of the city clerk to be
printed as the designated addressee of the Application for Absent
Voter's Ballot which is a separate document and can be detached
from the Sample Ballot for use as a self-mailer.

Exhibit pages were previously transmitted to you on February 26, 1990, by
facsimile transmission.

Your confirmation is needed by Friday, March 9, 1990. This will allow the
election consultant sufficient time to print the Sample Ballots. My facsimile
telephone number is: 1-213-513-6243.

Thank you for your prompt attention.

Sincerely,

Helen S. Kawagoe, CMC
City Clerk

CITY HALL • 701 EAST CARSON STREET • P.O. BOX 6234 • CARSON, CALIFORNIA 90749 • (213)-830-7600

PERMANENT ABSENT VOTER STATUS

Any voter who has lost, or has lost use of, one or more limbs, has lost use of both hands, is unable to move without the aid of an assistant device (e.g. cane, crutches, walker, wheelchair), is suffering from lung disease, has a significant limitation of the use of the lower extremities, or is suffering from a diagnosed disease or disorder which substantially impairs or interferes with the person's mobility may apply for Permanent Absent Voter Status.


You may request an application for Permanent Absent Voter Status from the COUNTY ELECTION DEPARTMENT.


From:

_______________________________

_______________________________

_______________________________


[1st Class
Postage
Place Stamp Here]


HELEN S. KAWAGOE, CITY CLERK ?

TO: OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK
CITY HALL, 701 E. CARSON STREET
P.O. BOX 6234
CARSON, CALIFORNIA 90749

HELEN S. KAWAGOE, CITY CLERK
OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK
CITY HALL, 701 E. CARSON STREET
P. O. BOX 6234
CARSON, CALIFORNIA 90749
(213) 830-7600, EXT. 275

BULK RATE
U.S. POSTAGE

PAID
Long Beach, CA
Permit No. 908

POLLS OPEN AT 7 A.M. AND CLOSE AT 8 P.M.

WHEN POLLING PLACE IS INACCESSIBLE TO THE HANDICAPPED,
BALLOT MAY BE VOTED OUTSIDE THE POLLING PLACE*

1600
Ballot Type

↓…………………..TEAR ON PERFORATED LINE AND ATTACH POSTAGE TO REVERSE SIDE…………………↓

APPLICATION FOR ABSENT VOTER'S BALLOT
DO NOT USE THIS FORM IF YOU HAVE ALREADY REQUESTED AN ABSENT VOTER BALLOT FOR THIS ELECTION

I hereby request an absentee ballot for the
CITY OF CARSON
GENERAL MUNICIPAL ELECTION
APRIL 10, 1990

APRIL 3, 1990

PRINT NAME AS REGISTERED _______________________________________________________________

LAST DAY APPLICATION MAY BE RECEIVED BY CLERK

REGISTERED RESIDENCE ADDRESS ___________________________________________________________________

CITY AND ZIP _________________________________________________________________ PHONE # _______________

MAIL ABSENTEE BALLOT TO ________________________________________________________________________

SIGNATURE OF APPLICANT X _____________________________________________________ DATE __________________

Voters with unaffiliated disabilities may qualify as PERMANENT ABSENT VOTERS. Contact your local county clerk or registrar of voters for further information.

FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY!   Signature Checked ______   Match (No Match) _____

Precinct No. _____ Ballot No. _____ Ballot Group No. _____ Ballot Group No. _____

Date Ballot Mailed or Voted in Office _________ Date Ballot Returned _________

Signature on Envelope vs. Application _______ Match (No Match) By _________

Precinct No. and Handicapped Accessibility
Polling Place Description
Polling Place Address
Ballot Group No. and Microfilm Access No.

DELIVER TO:

[illegible section at bottom]

CITY OF

CARSON

OFFICIAL
SAMPLE BALLOT
and Voter Information Pamphlet

GENERAL MUNICIPAL ELECTION
TUESDAY, APRIL 10, 1990

Compiled and Prepared by

OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK

HELEN S. KAWAGOR, CITY CLERK

POLLS OPEN AT 7 A.M. AND CLOSE AT 8 P.M.

NOTICE: ABSENT VOTER BALLOT APPLICATION ENCLOSED

CENSUS '90

THE LOCATION OF YOUR POLLING PLACE
IS SHOWN AT BOTTOM OF BACK COVER

2025
0435

CITY OF CARSON
701 East Carson Street • Post Office Box 6234
Carson, California 90745
Switchboard (213) 830-7600
Telecopier (213) 513-6243

TELECOPY COVER SHEET

FPPC
Attention: John Wallace
Legal Division
TO:__________________________________ FROM:________Wanda Higaki______________

TELEPHONE: 1-916-327-2026                TOTAL PAGES (including this sheet) 4_______

OUR FILE NO.: _________________________ YOUR FILE NO.: _______________________

SUBJECT: Mass Mailings Sent at Public Expense (Sec. 1890I)     DATE: 2/26/90___________

DOCUMENT TELECOPIED: Excerpt Pages: City of Carson Sample Ballot and Voter Information
                     Pamphlet and Absent Voter Ballot Application

REMARKS: ________________________________________________________________

See below.

________________________________________________________________________

________________________________________________________________________


THE CITY OF CARSON USES A XEROX MODEL 295 AUTOMATIC TELECOPIER WHICH IS
COMPATIBLE WITH MOST GROUP 1, 2, & 3 TELECOPIER MACHINES. IF YOU HAVE
DIFFICULTY RECEIVING ANY PAGES, PLEASE TELEPHONE THE CITY CLERK'S OFFICE
AT (213) 830-7600, EXTENSION 275.


In reference to the new interpretation that the sample ballot is a form of the agency, the following
inquiries are raised relative to Section 18901(b)(1):

1. Is it permissible for the name of the City Clerk to appear on the face sheet of the Sample
   Ballot (Exh. A)?

2. Is it permissible for the name of the City Clerk to appear as the return addressee of the Sample
   Ballot (Exh. B)?

3. Is it permissible for the name of the City Clerk to appear as the addressee of the Application
   for Absent Voter's Ballot (Exh. C)? Please notice that the Application for Absent Voter's
   Ballot is detachable from the Sample Ballot as a separate document for use as a self-mailer.

Thank you for your time and consideration. If it would be more expedient, please indicate your
remarks on the exhibit pages and have your secretary "fax" your response to me.



Disposition:      Original: City Clerk      Pink: Department

embedding.qa_text: This letter is from the City of Carson, dated February 27, 1990, addressed to Mr. John Wallace at the Fair Political Practices Commission in Sacramento, CA. The letter requests confirmation regarding the placement of the city clerk's name on various documents related to sample ballots and absentee voter applications. The key points discussed in the letter are: 1. It would not be allowable for the name of the city clerk to be printed on the cover sheet page of the Sample Ballot. 2. It would be allowable for the name of the city clerk to be printed as the return addressee of the Sample Ballot. 3. It would be allowable for the name of the city clerk to be printed as the designated addressee of the Application for Absent Voter's Ballot, which is a separate document and can be detached from the Sample Ballot for use as a self-mailer. Exhibit pages were previously transmitted to Mr. Wallace on February 26, 1990, by facsimile transmission. Your confirmation is needed by Friday, March 9, 1990, to allow the election consultant sufficient time to print the Sample Ballots. The facsimile telephone number provided is: 1-213-513-6243. Thank you for your prompt attention. Sincerely, Helen S. Kawagoe, CMC City Clerk PERMANENT ABSENT VOTER STATUS Any voter who has lost, or has lost use of, one or more limbs, has lost use of both hands, is unable to move without the aid of an assistant device (e.g. cane, crutches, walker, wheelchair), is suffering from lung disease, has a significant limitation of the use of the lower extremities, or is suffering from a diagnosed disease or disorder which substantially impairs or interferes with the person's mobility may apply for Permanent Absent Voter Status. You may request an application for Permanent Absent Voter Status from the COUNTY ELECTION DEPARTMENT. From: TO: HELEN S. KAWAGOE, CITY CLERK OFFICE OF THE CITY CLERK CITY HALL, 701 E. CARSON STREET P.O. BOX 6234 CARSON, CALIFORNIA 90749 This document is an application form for an absent voter's ballot, used in the City of Carson General Municipal Election on April 10, 1990. The form includes fields for the applicant's name as registered, registered residence address, city and zip code, phone number, and mailing address for the absentee ballot. There is also a section for the signature of the applicant and a note about permanent absentee voters. The form is signed by Helen S. Kawagoe, City Clerk, and indicates that ballots may be voted outside the polling place if the polling place is inaccessible to the handicapped. The form is stamped with a bulk rate U.S. Postage stamp and has a permit number from Long Beach, CA. This document is an official sample ballot and voter information pamphlet for the City of Carson, California. It is for the General Municipal Election held on Tuesday, April 10, 1990. The ballot was compiled and prepared by the Office of the City Clerk, with Helen S. Kawagoe as the City Clerk. The pamphlet states that polls open
